title: Blog
description: Articles, notes, and build logs about software engineering, product delivery, and practical implementation work.

posts.title: Deploying a Next.js Blog with Supabase
posts.excerpt: A practical setup for a public blog that stays fast, SEO-friendly, and free to host.
posts.contentHtml:
This setup keeps the public site on Next.js and Vercel, while Supabase stores the article data and uploaded media. That gives you a clean editor flow without paying for a separate CMS.

What matters most

  • Server-rendered article pages for Google indexing
  • Rich text editor support for real writing work
  • Supabase Storage for cover images and inline assets
  • One public route per article slug

When you are ready to publish, the editor writes directly into Supabase. The front end simply reads published posts and renders them with a dedicated article layout.
posts.seoDescription: How to run a free public blog on Next.js, Vercel, and Supabase with SEO-ready article pages.
posts.author: Dzaki Amri Zaidaan

posts.title: Writing Cleaner Content for Recruiters
posts.excerpt: A content strategy that keeps portfolio writing focused, credible, and easy to scan.
posts.contentHtml:
Good technical writing should be easy to scan, specific enough to build trust, and structured so readers can find the point quickly.

Practical rules

  1. Lead with the problem and the outcome
  2. Use one idea per paragraph
  3. Keep examples concrete and short
  4. Let screenshots and images support the narrative

That pattern works well for a portfolio blog because it helps both humans and search engines understand what the article is about.
posts.seoDescription: Practical writing rules for a portfolio blog that serves both recruiters and search engines.
posts.author: Dzaki Amri Zaidaan
